payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenues were $6.2 million favorable to the Forecast due to increased non-commutation ridership as well as higher average yield per passenger on East of Hudson lines partially offset by lower commutation ridership on East of Hudson lines and lower average yield per passenger on West of Hudson lines.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. The current month's actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

tool_args.label: Steven Weiss presented as Financial Liaison

payload.claim_text: Total Positions 6,880 forecast vs 6,705 actual, 175 favorable variance. Maintenance had 286 favorable variance (3,965 forecast vs 3,679 actual). Operations had 166 unfavorable (2,276 forecast vs 2,442 actual). Administration had 47 favorable (566 forecast vs 519 actual).
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

payload.claim_text: Labor Expenses: $15.3 million favorable to the Forecast. Payroll $4.5M favorable due to decreased hiring activity and lower Retro-Wage accruals. Health & Welfare $7.3M favorable due to lower rates. Pensions $7.1M favorable reflecting lower rates. Overtime $0.8M unfavorable due to higher scheduled service and weather emergencies.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

payload.claim_text: Non-Labor Expenses: $3.0 million favorable to the Forecast. Professional Service Contracts $9.8M favorable due to a true-up for IT allocations and timing of consulting/engineering services. Maintenance and Other Operating Contracts $5.0M unfavorable. Electric Power $1.3M unfavorable due to higher traction rates. Claims $3.2M unfavorable reflecting higher passenger claim provision.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad
